response_text:
To find out how many telephone calls Jason needs to make, we can work backward from his goal using the ratios provided:

1.  **Determine how many customers need to visit the dealership:**
    Jason knows that 1 out of every 2 customers who visit will buy a car (a 2:1 ratio).
    $15 \text{ cars} \times 2 \text{ customers per car} = 30 \text{ customers needed}.$

2.  **Determine how many telephone calls are needed to get those customers:**
    Jason knows that for every 25 telephone calls, 1 person visits the dealership.
    $30 \text{ customers} \times 25 \text{ calls per customer} = 750 \text{ calls}.$

**Jason would need to make 750 telephone calls to sell 15 cars.**
